name: Creamed Scallops in Wine Sauce (Coquilies St. Jacques)
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Can mushroom stems & pieces, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. If scallops are frozen, partially defrost according to package directions.
2. In a deep, 1 1/2-quart, heat-resistant, non-metallic casserole dish, place the 1 tablespoon butter or margarine and onion. Heat, uncovered, in Microwave Oven for 1 minute or until onion is tender.
3. Add scallops, lemon juice, salt, marjoram, paprika, and white wine. Stir to combine. Cover and heat in Microwave Oven for 3 minutes.
4. Add mushrooms and heat, covered, in Microwave Oven for 1 minute or until scallops are tender. Do not overcook scallops as they will become tough.
5. Drain liquid and reserve. Set scallops and liquid aside.
6. Melt the 3 tablespoons of butter or margarine in a small, heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl in Microwave Oven for 30 seconds. Blend in flour.
7. Gradually stir in reserved scallop liquid and cream.
8. Heat, uncovered, in Microwave Oven for 2 minutes or until thickened and smooth. Stir in parsley.
9. Combine reserved scallop mixture and sauce. Spoon the mixture into 4 serving shells or small non-metallic ramekins.
10. Cover and heat, uncovered, in Microwave Oven for 3 minutes. If browning is desired, place under broiler of conventional oven several minutes or until lightly browned.
